title: The Bustle In A House
content:
The bustle in a house
    The morning after death
    Is solemnest of industries
    Enacted upon earth, --

    The sweeping up the heart,
    And putting love away
    We shall not want to use again
    Until eternity.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Bustle In A House"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
